feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Upgrade Role in SAP S/4HANA On-Premise (SAP GUI only)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

- After upgrading SAP S/4HANA 2021 to SAP S/4HANA 2023, will the roles on the 3-System be upgraded automatically or manually?

- How do I get a list of obsolete T-codes / new T-codes / new authorization objects?

Please help to advise

Thanks,
QuocDT5

entry.title: Leveraging Joule Custom Skills in S/4HANA PCE: A Clean Core Approach for Sourcing & Procurement
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

As a consultant focused on RISE with SAP and Clean Core strategies, I’ve been exploring how Joule (SAP’s Generative AI) can transform the user experience within S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (PCE), specifically for Sourcing & Procurement.

Integrating a custom skill developed in SAP Build with the Fiori Launchpad (S/4HANA 2025) is more than just a technical milestone; it is a strategic shift. For those of us managing large-scale environments, this represents a powerful way to handle extensibility Side-by-Side on SAP BTP, keeping the core stable and upgrade-ready.

Key Insights from my recent implementation:

  1. Enhancing Procurement Efficiency: By leveraging custom skills, we can empower users to query complex contract details or PR/PO statuses through natural language, significantly reducing the volume of L1/L2 support tickets.

  2. Clean Core Alignment: Using SAP Build Actions to consume OData APIs ensures that we are following SAP Best Practices, avoiding legacy "Z" developments inside the S/4HANA core.

  3. The SAP Activate Connection: During the Run phase, Joule acts as a continuous enabler, helping users navigate business processes without leaving the Fiori environment.

I’d love to hear your thoughts: For those already working with Joule in S/4HANA 2025 PCE, how are you handling the governance of these custom skills? Are you prioritizing "Read-only" informational skills, or are you moving towards transactional "Write" scenarios (e.g., creating Purchase Requisitions) in production?

Looking forward to exchanging experiences on this AI journey!

#RISEwithSAP #S4HANA #SAPMM #CleanCore #Joule #SAPBuild #BTP #SourcingAndProcurement

entry.title: ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINTにおける「FOR <sql_statement>」の指定方法について
entry.content:
現在、現行環境と比較して新環境の処理時間が遅く、業務に支障が生じており、
新環境を本番稼働できない可能性があるという大きな課題があります。
 
このため、処理時間が遅いSQL文に対してHINT句を指定する方法について調査を行っています。
 
SAP Note DIGITS の「6. 文のヒントはどのように管理できますか。」にて、
以下のSQLコマンドの説明があることを確認しました。
ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINT ( <hints> ) [OVERRIDE] [COMMENT '<comment>'] FOR <sql_statement>
 
本コマンドについて、以下の２点をご教示いただけますでしょうか。
 
１．FOR <sql_statement> の <sql_statement> には、STATEMENT HASH ではなく、SQL文そのものを指定することは可能でしょうか。
 
２．（上記 1 において SQL文を指定可能な場合）
　　<sql_statement> に、以下の例のように % などのワイルドカードを指定することは可能でしょうか。
　　目的としては、1回のコマンドで複数のSQL文に対してHINT句を指定できるかを確認したいと考えています。
 
　　ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINT ( USE_HEX_PLAN ) FOR 'SELECT % FROM "Z_CDS_VIEW_A" WHERE %'
 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Exit or enhancement option for Tasklist Component Assignment
entry.content: We are currently investigating the feasibility of adding custom fields to the Component Assignments subscreen (Screen 3500) within the General Task List operation (Program SAPLCMDI). Preliminary analysis via transaction SMOD and searching for relevant BAdIs has not yielded a standard enhancement option for this specific screen. Could you please confirm if there is a supported SAP standard exit, Business Add-In, or enhancement spot available to facilitate this requirement without modification to the standard object?

entry.title: Obsolete Tcode/ new Tcode after upgrading SAP S/4HANA On-Premise (SAP GUI only)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

After upgrading SAP S/4HANA 2021 to SAP S/4HANA 2023:

- How do I get a list of obsolete T-codes / new T-codes / new authorization objects?

Please help to advise

Thanks,
QuocDT5

entry.title: Dynamic Control in Custom Flexible Workflow
entry.content: Hello everyone, I'm currently doing a custom Flexible Workflow that requires dynamic approver based on 3rd party API data. Are there any ways for customizing the User decisions/Activities using ABAP code in callback class?

entry.title: ‘Plant’ filed not available in step condition in Manage workflow for Lean Service app
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We have a requirement to add an additional step condition “Plant” in the app Manage Workflow for Service Entry – Lean Services in S/4HANA Private Cloud.

To achieve this, we implemented the following BAdIs as suggested by SAP documentation:

SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF,  SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL

The Plant field is successfully added as a workflow condition in the Manage Workflow app. However, during Service Entry Sheet creation and submission for approval, the workflow is not triggering based on the maintained Plant condition.

We have verified:

  • The workflow configuration is active.

  • The condition is maintained correctly in the workflow definition.

  • The BAdI implementations are active.

    Despite this, the Plant-based condition is not being evaluated at runtime.

    Has anyone faced a similar issue, or are there any additional steps/checks required for Plant-based condition evaluation in Lean Services workflow?

    Any guidance would be highly appreciated.

    Thanks in advance.

    Regards,

    Gaurav W

entry.title: Combined Chart Column & Line with different dimentions on s4 hana analytical report
entry.content:
Hi all, Im a developer, not a bi/bw expert

i've created a Cube and a Query on S4hana, after that i've created an analytical report with some graphics and it is working well. ( Using app manage KPI and Reports )

My requirement is to have a combined chart, Collum and Line, but the line is just to have the total, so different dimentions :

So Columns are PLANT/DATE. Line shoud be only DATE ( to display the total )

Currently when im creating the combined it displays Column and Line, but with same values

 

What i need is only 1 line with total. Is it possible to create such graphic on Analytical list page?

current graphic configuration

S4 Hana 2025 ( we dont have SAC )

entry.title: PPDS: On the detailed scheduling dashboard - Fixing Interval
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a scenario where users add the fixed intervals on the dashboard for 2 weeks.
i,e orders within the 2 week fixed interval cannot be rescheduled. 
However when they execute the process orders in s/4 hana with re-read master data or switch source of supply from the Dashboard (on the same line/resource), system is moving/re-scheduling these orders out of the fixing interval. Even if the order is Fixed/Firmed.

How can we switch source of supply without disturbing the existing schedule of the fixed order.

entry.title: S/4 Hana - Track on SO schedule line level both requested delivery date and committed delivery date
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am working for a Make to Order scenario project, my customer wants to trace in the sales order just for statistical purpose at schedule line level both  the initial requested delivery date of the customer and also the committed date (if the date requested by the customer is not feasible).

In the sales order there is not availability check and are not configured lead times; the Customer care should enter manually the requested delivery date for each sales order item (also eventually splitting quantity on different dates), then if after discussion with Planning the date is not feasible the Customer care will enter for each schedule line previously defined the committed date (the date on which the company commits to the customer to deliver the product).

There is any standard solution to satisfy this requirement? Otherwise I am thinking to add a new custom field "committed date" on schedule line tab of the sales order.

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: POST Call for UI_MAINTWORKREQUESTOVW_V2 with C_MaintWorkRequestOverviewTP Entity gives Forbidden
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to test POST call for UI_MAINTWORKREQUESTOVW_V2 with C_MaintWorkRequestOverviewTP and to_EventPrio Navigation for Priority creation but its giving Dump and Forbidden error on Gateway client but same works as expected on "Screen Maintenance Request" application.

We are having Maintenance notification created and when we Edit it and tries to add Priorities POST call works and gives created response in Network tab.
Same when I trying on Gateway client its giving Forbidden and Short dump below

Can someone help me to find what exactly I am doing wrong?

Thank you,

Ankita

entry.title: Clarification Needed: Functional Status of MRP Type V1 in ECC/S/4HANA Production
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am seeking clarification regarding the functional status and production usage of MRP Type V1 (Manual Reorder Point Planning with External Requirements) in current SAP ECC and/or S/4HANA environments.

We are evaluating consumption-based planning methods for externally procured operating supplies with the following characteristics:

  • Seasonal demand pattern

  • Primarily, break-in (reactive) maintenance consumption

  • No advance planned independent requirements

  • Variable replenishment lead time (observed 9–17 days)

  • Forecast model currently set to “N”

During internal review, it was suggested that MRP Type V1 may not be fully functional or widely supported in production systems. Since V1 remains selectable in the material master (MRP 1 view), we would like to confirm its current technical status.

Specific questions:

  1. Is MRP Type V1 fully supported and functional in active ECC and S/4HANA production environments?

  2. Are there configuration dependencies (MRP group settings, external requirements inclusion, planning file settings, etc.) that would cause V1 not to trigger as expected?

  3. Has V1 behavior changed in S/4HANA compared to ECC?

  4. Is V1 actively used in production by SAP customers for consumption-based planning?

Our objective is to verify whether V1 is a supported and operational planning method before proceeding with configuration changes.

Any clarification, OSS references, or real-world implementation insights would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: About EWM Note 2464460 (SAP-internal test purposes only)
entry.content:
In note 2464460 BC sets listed for embedded EWM are marked “SAP-internal test purposes only",
Does it indicates that these BC sets are not intended to be used as a standard, default configuration baseline for implementing a productive embedded EWM warehouse.?
if not then which BC sets should be considered to proceed implementing embedded EWM on S4HANA. 

Thank You
Kalyan

entry.title: S/4HANA 2023 (202302 FPS): Missing entries in table FINS_TRR (EBRR customizing) – how to initialize
entry.content:
Hi all,
we are introducing SAP S/4HANA Service (no Advanced Execution) and want to use a minimal FI/CO integration based on Item-Based Accounting (IBA) and Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR).

While validating the configuration, we noticed that table FINS_TRR (Event-Based Revenue Recognition: Customizing) is missing expected entries / appears incomplete in our client. Because of this, we cannot properly verify or complete some EBRR configuration steps for Service Documents (e.g., deriving/assigning a revenue recognition key for service documents), or we seem to be missing standard reference data.

Is there any documentation guide or how-to?

Best regards,
Lars

entry.title: Standard Configuration posible TECO is not allowed without closing the inspection lot
entry.content:
Process Order Quality inspection is active status; user cannot be able to do TECO.
QA inspection UD Passed stock posting pending also TECO not allow.

entry.title: Automatically exporting invoice data to spreadsheet
entry.content:
We currently manually export data from Fiori apps to Excel and then upload it manually in SharePoint. 

Is there a way to automate this without manual intervention? It wastes a lot of time for user to do this manually. 

How can we automate this export of SAP data?

  1. MIR5
    1. Filter by Invoice Status Parked and Entry Type Parked
    2. Export spreadsheet and save in GPM folder

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Best Practice for Handling Multiple SAP Backends in UI5 Apps Without Hardcoding URLs
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently working on SAP Fiori / UI5 applications that communicate with multiple SAP backend systems via OData, and I’m looking for best practices to avoid hardcoding URLs or system-specific destinations in the app configuration.

Our system landscape contains several backend systems, for example:

  • Production: F51, F52, F53
  • Development / Sandbox: D5W, S5W

Previously, in an on-premise setup, we handled this using system aliases. In UI5 apps or destinations, we referenced something like:

 

destination: D5W_Principal_Propagation

The alias/destination pointed to the appropriate backend system, so we did not need to hardcode real URLs.

 

Current Challenge

With BTP destinations, we want to completely avoid hardcoding URLs in ui5.yaml or in the application code. However, in our scenario:

  • There are multiple backend systems per environment
  • Each system may expose its own OData services
  • Our apps previously relied on alias/destination names to choose the target system

Example of current configuration:

 

backend:
  - path: /sap
    url: http://................................
    destination: D5W_Principal_Propagation

This is DEV-specific and would require manual changes for other systems — which we want to eliminate.

 

Options I’m Considering

 Create one destination per backend system

Example: EWM_F51, EWM_F52, EWM_D5W, etc.

→ The app selects the destination dynamically based on context

 Use one destination per environment and let ABAP/Gateway system aliases handle routing

→ UI5 app calls one logical destination (for example EWM_MAIN)

→ Backend decides which system (F51/F52/F53) processes the request

 Use external configuration to avoid hardcoding destination names in the UI5 app

 

Questions

  • What is the recommended best practice when you have multiple backend systems in the same environment?
  • Is it better to create multiple destinations or rely on backend alias routing?
  • What are best practices for dynamic destination selection in UI5 apps?
  • How do you avoid hardcoding URLs and destination names in large multi-system landscapes?

Any guidance, architecture recommendations, or real project experience would be very helpful.

Thank you.

entry.title: Note 2267878 - Parameter Effectivity has been hidden for Classification
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need to migrate from SAP R/3 to SAP S/4HANA.

While reviewing the simplification items, specifically SAP Note 2267878 (“Parameter Effectivity has been hidden for Classification”), I don’t clearly understand what has changed or what “parameter effectivity” for classification actually refers to.

What activities need to be completed before the migration?

Could someone please explain this simplification item in detail?

Thanks

AL

entry.title: SAP Ariba Invoicing / SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition?
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

The Process Navigator and SAP Help contain references stating that SAP Ariba Invoicing (formerly SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management) is also available for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. However, there does not appear to be an official price list for this. In short: Is SAP Ariba Invoicing now available for the Private Edition or not? If not, when will it be available?

Best regards,

Patrick

entry.title: POs are not transferred to accruals engine for SES accruals
entry.content:
Dear Members
I have configured Purchase Order Accruals (POAC) functionality in SAP S/4HANA 2023 version and is working fine. I also configured SES Accruals (SSAC). Raised a PO (ME21N) with account assignment category K and Item category D with a limit. PO date 01.02.2026 and the delivery date is 30.04.2026. I raised a service entry sheet via tcode ML81N with posting date 20.02.2026. When I run ACEPOSTINGRUN (with date 28.02.2026) this PO is not getting captured. 
My questions are
  1. Should I raise the service entry sheet via Fiori App Manage Service Entry Sheets - Lean services (F2027) for SES accruals to trigger?
  2. If yes to the above, can I raise a SES via Fiori app F2027 against a PO raised via ME21N? 
Thanks
Kera
